title: Uninstall legacy CFW (CBHC / Indexiine / PayloadLoader)
requirements: A Wii U with a legacy CFW you want to remove
An SD card formatted as FAT32 (a microSD with an adapter is fine)
warnings: Uninstall in the RIGHT order. For CBHC especially, you MUST remove CBHC before touching the injected DS game, or you will brick.
Only follow the section that matches what you actually installed — skip the others with Next.
_note: Transcribed from wiiu.hacks.guide uninstall-cbhc / uninstall-indexiine / uninstall-payloadloader. Three independent procedures in one guide; skip the steps that don't apply to you.

steps.title: Which one are you removing?
steps.body:
This guide covers three legacy uninstalls. Follow **only** the section that matches your setup and press **Next** to skip the rest:

- **CBHC** — the coldboot Haxchi that autoboots into CFW.
- **Indexiine** — the browser home-page mod that auto-launches the Homebrew Launcher.
- **PayloadLoader** — Aroma's PayloadLoader injected into Health & Safety.

steps.title: Uninstall CBHC
steps.body:
Press the button to download the **Homebrew Launcher** and the **CBHC installer** onto your SD card, then on the Wii U:

1. Launch the **Homebrew Launcher**.
2. Open the **CBHC Installer**.
3. Select the DS Virtual Console game you want to remove CBHC from, press **A**.
4. Read the warnings, then press **B**.
5. Reboot and confirm the Wii U **no longer autoboots** into CBHC (no boot menu appears).
6. Once it boots straight to the Wii U Menu, open **System Settings → Data Management** and safely delete the app called **DON'T TOUCH ME**.

If you get a system-memory error deleting `DON'T TOUCH ME`, restart and try again. Only after this is CBHC fully gone and the DS game safe to remove.

action.label: Download CBHC tools to SD card

steps.title: Uninstall Indexiine
steps.body:
Press the button to download the **Homebrew Launcher** and the **Indexiine Installer** onto your SD card, then on the Wii U:

1. Insert the card and launch the **Homebrew Launcher** through the Internet Browser (wait a few seconds).
2. Open the **Indexiine Installer** and press **B** to uninstall.
3. Restart and confirm the Homebrew Launcher no longer auto-launches when you open the browser.

action.label: Download Indexiine tools to SD card

steps.title: PayloadLoader: undo autoboot first
steps.body:
If your Wii U autoboots into the PayloadLoader/Aroma, undo that before removing it:

1. Launch the **PayloadLoader Installer** from the Wii U Menu.
2. Press **A** to select **Check**.
3. Select **Boot options**.
4. When asked, press **A** to select **Switch back to Wii U Menu**.
5. When it finishes, press **A** to shut down.

The console now boots to the Wii U Menu; the PayloadLoader can still be reached via the **Health & Safety** app.

steps.title: PayloadLoader: remove it
steps.body:
Press the button to download the latest Aroma payloads and base files onto your SD card, then on the Wii U:

1. Launch the **Health & Safety** app and boot into Aroma.
2. Open the **PayloadLoader Installer** from the Wii U Menu.
3. Press **A** to select **Check**.
4. Select **Remove**.
5. When asked to confirm, select **Remove** again.
6. When it finishes, press **A** to shut down.

Note: a factory reset does **not** remove the injected PayloadLoader — this is the only way to fully uninstall it.

action.label: Download Aroma files to SD card

steps.title: You're done
steps.body: The legacy CFW you targeted is removed. If you uninstalled CBHC, you can now safely uninstall/reinstall the DS Virtual Console game from the eShop.
